Recovery / SUD Services

  • Full assessment for care needs
  • Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)
  • Individual & group counseling
  • Case management
  • Housing assistance
  • Transportation

Help is available for individuals experiencing substance use disorder (SUD).

If you or someone you care about is seeking treatment for SUD, we offer resources and support to help navigate the recovery process. Our specialists provide personalized, evidence-based care and work with each patient to identify the treatment options that best meet their needs.

Outpatient Care

Phone: (209) 940-5662 1031 Waterloo Rd, Stockton CA 95205 200 Cottage Ave #104, Manteca CA 95336 730 N. Central Ave, Tracy CA 95376 201 N. Stanislaus St #101, Stockton CA 95202

Residential Treatment

Phone: (209) 762-5000 201 N. Stanislaus St #102, Stockton CA 95202

Learn more

Stay informed about events, programs, and updates! Follow CMCenters on social media or subscribe to our e-news below.
  • CMC Respite Center and Tuleburg Press partner to help patients heal through journaling
  • Open House to Introduce Manteca Recovery Center
  • Growing youth program tackles behavioral-health and SUD challenges

Locations Offering This Service

  • CMC Manteca 200 Cottage Ave, Manteca, CA 95336 (209) 624-5800
  • CMC Respite Center (Medical) 201 N Stanislaus St, Stockton, CA 95202 (209) 762-5000
  • CMC Tracy Central 730 N. Central Ave, Tracy CA 95376 (209) 650-4000
  • CMC Waterloo Recovery Center 1031 Waterloo Rd, Stockton, CA 95205 (209) 940-5662

For Stockton Residents

Mobile Community Response: In partnership with the City of Stockton, Community Medical Centers offers an alternative to 911 for Stockton citizens in need of behavioral health services. If you or someone you know is experiencing mental distress, please call (833) 311-2273, 7 days a week, 8:00 am to 11:00 pm. This helpline is for non-violent individuals with mild to moderate symptoms.
